elements.name: Title
texts: "I reached one of their towns called Tearmann"

elements.name: Description
texts: A famous account of a 1398 visit by Viscount Ramon de Perellós to Saint Patrick's Purgatory

elements.name: Source
texts: The Journey of Viscount Ramon de Perellós to Saint Patrick's Purgatory, p. 10

elements.name: Contributor
texts: CELT: Corpus of Electronic Texts: a project of University College, Cork. Translated by Alan Mac an Bhaird.

elements.name: Rights
texts: Citation for the purposes of criticism

elements.name: Text
texts: "I departed from that king's court and followed the road for several days — because the road is long — until I reached one of their towns called Tearmann and they call it thus because there they will do no harm to anyone; it would seem they have great devotion to Saint Patrick and it is good through the realm; and the kings keep that town safe and the pilgrims who go there are obliged to leave their animals there for horses or other animals could not cross the mountains or the waters. "

elements.name: Original Format
texts: The Journey of Viscount Ramon De Perellós to Saint Patrick's Purgatory. Alan Mac an Bhaird (ed), First edition [i + 23 pp] CELT Cork (2012)
